creator: rsmith@xs4all.nl
text:
In a discussion on the freebsd-stable list, see e.g. message-id
	201001091322.o09DMjgZ041660@lurza.secnetix.de it turned out that old
	binaries were not always properly deleted when the delete-old* targets
	were used.  I decided to put some effort into updating
	tools/build/mk/OptionalObsoleteFiles.inc, starting from the version
	that came with 8.1-RELEASE. I've only added stuff that I
	traced by running 'make -n install' in relevant directories, so I
	skipped stuff like GSSAPI and KERBEROS that I'm not familiar enough
	with or which are spread out over different places. I sorted the list
	to keep it in the same sequence as the targets mentioned in
	src.conf(5) and added some placeholders where relevant.

Fix: The xz compressed patch file OptionalObsoleteFiles.patch.xz is
	enclosed in uuencoded form below



Apply the abovementioned patch.--MgX7r1I6vEP0i0kWi2mkLi6ZWu80ZnvKC3gUH15UeN7mvODy
Content-Type: text/plain; name="OptionalObsoleteFiles.patch.xz"
Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit
Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="OptionalObsoleteFiles.patch.xz"

begin 644 OptionalObsoleteFiles.patch.xz

How-To-Repeat: 	E.g. if the system is built WITHOUT_NTP, old binaries and files
	related to ntp are not properly deleted by the 'make delete-old*'
	targets. This PR aims to be a start at fixing that.
